LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BRENDA JACKSON,AND PEARLIE JAMES'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR. CROSS-DEFENDANTS SEEK JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS PER CCP 438. CCP 439(A) REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES SHALL "MEET IN CONFER IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE" PRIOR TO FILING THE MOTION. CCP 439(A)(3) ALSO REQUIRES MOVANT TO PROVIDE A "DECLARATION" REGARDING THE MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. HERE, MOVANT MERELY SENT A LETTER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS AND CLAIMS THERE WAS NO RESPONSE. MOVANT ALSO FAILS TO PROVIDE A "DECLARATION" REGARDING THE MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice And Motion For Summary Judgment; And Memorandum Of Points Authorities

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cross Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs and Cross-Defendants Brenda Jackson and Pearlie James' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cross-Complaint is OFF CALENDAR.

Cross-defendants seek judgment on the pleadings per Code of Civil Procedure section 438. Code of Civil Procedure section 439(a) requires that the parties shall "meet in confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference" prior to filing the motion. Section 439(a)(3) also requires movant to provide a "declaration" regarding the meet and confer efforts. Here, Moving Cross-Defendants have not demonstrated compliance with section 439's requirements, The Diggs declaration documents email correspondence only, which is non-compliant. The court issued a similar order in June 2025 in this case. The parties are ordered to comply with the code.

The court notes that purported oppositions filed before the moving papers will not be considered. The parties must follow the rules regarding submission of motions-related papers.

Moving Cross-Defendants are ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving Cross-Defendants failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for Moving Cross-Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BRENDA JACKSON,and PEARLIE JAMES's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cross-Complaint.

Off calendar. Cross-defendants seek judgment on the pleadings per CCP 438. CCP 439(a) requires that the parties shall "meet in confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference" prior to filing the motion. CCP 439(a)(3) also requires movant to provide a "declaration" regarding the meet and confer efforts. Here, movant merely sent a letter to the cross-complainants and claims there was no response. Movant also fails to provide a "declaration" regarding the meet and confer efforts. The parties are ordered to comply with the code.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
